The daily grind

Neil Reisner The Miami Herald May 2000
Data & DatabasesInvestigative MethodsHealth & Environment

Driving computer-assisted reporting requires preparation, akin to practicing for a concert. It involves anticipating and analyzing available data to enhance reporting on deadline.

Driving CAR on deadline is not hard. It involves immense preparation. In CAR's case, knowing what data are available, how to analyze it, whom to call to verify it is crucial. CAR stories add context, turning simple stories into invaluable reports. Examples include analyzing FDA's 'Children & Tobacco Compliance Checker' database to measure state compliance levels and using FOIA requests for tobacco enforcement engagement.

Other features were made possible by practice and preparedness, like election precinct analyses or tracking gas prices, all aiding the process of consistent quality.
